documents.topic: SLI skip rationale for Array<Element>. Could be done (trivial self-carrier or parametric element-unwrap); skipped because trivial form is zero-payoff and parametric form pre-commits a specific Domain/Underlying choice that consumers should own.
SLI skip rationale for Set<Element>. Trivial form zero-payoff; parametric form has hash-consistency issues on element unwrap.
SLI skip rationale for Dictionary<Key, Value>. Two-parameter generic — can't map into single-param Carrier without axis choice that pre-commits semantics.

SLI skip rationale for Optional<Wrapped>. Strongest could-be-done case: parametric form reads cleanly (Optional.map-style). Skipped to avoid pre-committing Domain propagation choice for a type as central as Optional.

SLI skip rationale for Result<Success, Failure>. Sum type with no canonical wrapped value; Carrier's abstraction assumes single wrapped Underlying. Ecosystem prefers typed-throws-thunk over Result anyway (feedback_throws_not_result).
SLI skip rationale for Range / ClosedRange / PartialRangeFrom / PartialRangeThrough / PartialRangeUpTo. Interval types, not value wrappers; no canonical Underlying.
SLI skip rationale for Slice<Base>. View into another collection; semantic identity is 'view', not 'wrapped value'. Parallel to Array analysis plus the view-vs-wrapper mismatch.
SLI skip rationale for ContiguousArray<Element>. Same analysis as Array; contiguous-storage guarantee is orthogonal to Carrier.
SLI skip rationale for InlineArray<count, Element>. Array-family analysis plus the value-generic (count) axis that Carrier's abstraction doesn't parameterize over.
SLI skip rationale for Void / (). Structural blocker: Swift doesn't permit `extension Void: Carrier` (non-nominal type). A nominal Unit wrapper type would be invention, not stdlib integration.
SLI skip rationale for TaskPriority. Structurally viable (trivial self-carrier) but no demonstrated use case; too specialized for 0.1.0 FINAL commitment without concrete demand.

SLI skip rationale for the unsafe pointer family (UnsafePointer, UnsafeMutablePointer, UnsafeRawPointer, UnsafeMutableRawPointer, UnsafeBufferPointer, UnsafeMutableBufferPointer, OpaquePointer). Category error: pointers are reference (memory-address) types, not value wrappers. Carrier's abstraction would lie.

SLI skip rationale for Span / MutableSpan / RawSpan / MutableRawSpan. Strongest could-be-done case for ~Escapable Carrier conformance (Q3 quadrant). Skipped because the trivial-self-carrier default may not satisfy @_lifetime correctly for ~Escapable Self; each variant would need bespoke explicit-lifetime conformance.

SLI skip rationale for ContinuousClock.Instant and SuspendingClock.Instant. Positional semantics (point on a timeline) rather than value-wrapper semantics; weaker fit than Duration (which IS included). Too specialized to centralize without demand.

SLI skip rationale for Foundation types (Date, URL, Data, TimeInterval, UUID, and all others). Hard skip via [PRIM-FOUND-001]: primitives layer MUST NOT import Foundation. Any Foundation-type Carrier conformance belongs in a higher-layer package, not in carrier-primitives.

narrativeSections.heading: Provenance
narrativeSections.body: These four research documents were authored in swift-institute/Research/ on 2026-04-22 and moved to this package on 2026-04-24 when swift-carrier-primitives was created as the dedicated home for Carrier-family research and experiments. See swift-institute/Research/Reflections/2026-04-23-carrier-walkback-and-capability-lift-taxonomy.md for the full investigation arc, including the initial Carrier package proposal, walk-back on three grounds, and the 2026-04-24 revisit that produced this package.

narrativeSections.heading: Companion research
narrativeSections.body: The companion characterization of the orthogonal self-projection default pattern lives at swift-ownership-primitives/Research/self-projection-default-pattern.md (v1.0.0, 2026-04-24, RECOMMENDATION, Tier 2). The original authoring on 2026-04-22 was lost mid-session 2026-04-23 and re-authored on 2026-04-24 from the shipped Ownership.Borrow.`Protocol` source and the reflection record. The companion experiment (V0–V5 variants) was also lost and has not been re-authored; variant verdicts in the re-authored doc are replayed from shape analysis.
